INFO_102.title: Gender and Information Technology
INFO_102.description: Explores the social construction of gender in relation to the history and contemporary development of information technologies. Considers the importance of diversity and difference in the design and construction of innovative information technology solutions. Challenges prevailing viewpoints about who can and does work in the information technology field.

INFO_103.title: Social Media, Ethics, and Automation
INFO_103.description: Explores ethical concerns involving automation on social media platforms. Students learn about social media phenomena (e.g., viral memes, parasocial relationships, harassment campaigns), experiment with computer programs (e.g., bots, data mining programs, recommendation algorithms), and apply ethics frameworks (e.g., Taoism, virtue ethics, Ubuntu ethics). No prior programming experience assumed.

INFO_104.title: Technology Ethics, Policy, and Law
INFO_104.description: Drawing from law, ethics, and science and technology studies, introduces students to the ways contemporary society seeks to mitigate the harms of emerging technology and promote human flourishing.

INFO_180.title: Introduction to Data Science
INFO_180.description: Survey course introducing the essential elements of data science: data collection, management, curation, and cleaning; summarizing and visualizing data; basic ideas of statistical inference, machine learning. Students will gain hands-on experience through computing labs.

INFO_200.title: Intellectual Foundations of Informatics
INFO_200.description: Introduces the intellectual foundations of information, including what it is; how people create, categorize, find, interpret, manipulate and use information; how human values shape the design of information, information technology, and information systems; and how these systems shape people, organizations, and society. Includes analytic, design, empirical, and technical skill development.

INFO_201.title: Foundational Skills for Data Science
INFO_201.description: Introduces fundamental tools, technologies, and skills necessary to transform data into knowledge, including data manipulation, analysis, and visualization, as well as version control and programming languages used in data programming. Students learn to work with real data, and reflect on the power and perils of using data to inform.

INFO_270.title: Data Reasoning in a Digital World
INFO_270.description: Covers misinformation, causal fallacies, statistical traps, and refutation techniques...

INFO_310.title: Information Assurance and Cybersecurity
INFO_310.description: Introduction to information assurance and cybersecurity (IAC)...

INFO_330.title: Databases and Data Modeling
INFO_330.description: Introduction to database systems, relational models, and SQL...

INFO_351.title: Information Ethics and Policy
INFO_351.description: Analyzes ethical, legal, and policy issues surrounding information technologies...

INFO_370.title: Core Methods in Data Science
INFO_370.description: Surveys major topics within data science including machine learning and cloud computing...

INFO_371.title: Advanced Methods in Data Science
INFO_371.description: Emphasizes modern data science methods, machine learning, and experimental design...

INFO_376.title: Introduction to Search and Recommender Systems
INFO_376.description: Covers search engines, recommendation systems, and search interface technologies...

INFO_380.title: Product and Information Systems Management
INFO_380.description: Covers design of information systems and software, analyzing stakeholder needs...

INFO_430.title: Database Design and Management
INFO_430.description: Perspectives on DBMS theory, architecture, and implementation...

INFO_441.title: Server-Side Development
INFO_441.description: Introduces server-side web development programming, services, tools, and protocols...

INFO_448.title: Mobile Development: Android
INFO_448.description: Application development for Android devices using Java and XML...

INFO_449.title: Mobile Development: iOS
INFO_449.description: Application development for iOS devices using Swift and XML...

INFO_474.title: Interactive Information Visualization
INFO_474.description: Techniques and theory for visualizing and interacting with structured data...

INFO_490.title: Project Capstone I
INFO_490.description: First part of student-driven team project defining an information problem...

INFO_491.title: Project Capstone II
INFO_491.description: Continuation of student-driven team project, completing project deliverables...
